breakdown: This PR refactors rewards_box normalization to use a centralized robust numeric extraction pattern with multi-key fallbacks (e.g., xp_gained → xp → xp_awarded → etc.) and the _DNC.convert_value helper. The changes improve edge case handling for messy LLM hallucinated outputs (NaN, inf, empty strings, zero candidates). Minor issues: _get_raw returns Any losing type info, _DNC abbreviation is cryptic, and no explicit exception handling visible.
key_changes: Replaced inline coerce_int calls with centralized _get_raw(keys) helper that implements robust multi-key fallback strategy
Added _DNC.convert_value for centralized robust numeric conversion with fallback keys
Implemented _get_raw logic to handle edge cases: empty strings → skip, 0/0.0 → zero candidate, NaN/inf → skip, return first finite non-zero value
Added explanatory comments on WHY decisions (e.g., LLM non-standard boolean representations, treating empty as not present)
Created new test file testing_mcp/test_rewards_box_robustness.py with unit tests and E2E hallucination provocation tests
